label: GPUs, TPUs & AI accelerators

node.label: GPUs, TPUs & AI accelerators
node.definition: GPUs, TPUs, NPUs, and other AI accelerators execute dense, sparse, tensor, or event-driven operations with specialized memory hierarchies and interconnects.
node.why_it_matters: The role of GPUs, TPUs & AI accelerators is to connect Linear algebra, Numerical analysis to downstream capabilities including CUDA, ROCm, Triton & custom kernels, Quantized inference & compressed deployment, Edge, on-device & neuromorphic deployment.
examples: substrate for CUDA, ROCm, Triton & custom kernels
substrate for Quantized inference & compressed deployment
substrate for Edge, on-device & neuromorphic deployment
limitations: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Numerical analysis.

dependencies.rationale: GPUs, TPUs & AI accelerators turns Linear algebra into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
GPUs, TPUs & AI accelerators turns Numerical analysis into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
GPUs, TPUs & AI accelerators turns Distributed systems & networking into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
